### Post by theanswriz42 on 2007-01-17

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig

EndSection

EndSection

    Driver         "wacom"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

Section "InputDevice"

EndSection

EndSection

    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "ExactModeTimingsDVI" "TRUE"
    Option         "ModeValidation" "DFP-0: NoEdidDFPMaxSizeCheck, NoVesaModes"

        Modes      "1024x768"
    EndSubSection

        Modes      "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1024x768"

EndSection
```

And for the files I have installed on my system:

```
root@enceladus:/home/steve# dpkg -l | grep nvidia
ii  nvidia-glx                                 1.0.9629+2.6.17.6-2~amaranth         NVIDIA binary XFree86 4.x/X.Org driver
ii  nvidia-kernel-common                       20051028+1ubuntu7                    NVIDIA binary kernel module common files

```

So, my issue is such that my screen size is very small in the middle of my monitor with a black "box" around the edges. The good news is that the nvidia driver does load (I got the nvidia screen as i restart gdm) but I would like the viewable display to take the entire screen.

Cheers

Edit: Fixed "nv" for "nvidia" in this post.

### Post by Georges on 2007-02-12
what ist the monitor you are using?

Why this questions, well if your monitor is a 1600x1200 LCD and you display 1024x768 on it, it will show small with black border. If you use VGA instead of DVI the monitor usually zooms that to full size, but better is to tell X to use the LCD's resolution.

Just change the last line
   Modes      "1024x768"
to
   Modes      "1600x1200" "1024x768"

or whatever your LCD size is.

Georges

---

### Post by geoffrey99 on 2007-02-18
Had the same problem with an Acer TravelMate 630 and a Nvidia GeForce 2.

Screen was in a lack box or another way to put is centralised with a black border around it.

Started a terminal session, Applications, Accessories, Terminal and entered the command

sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Rebooted the machine and chose a high screen resolution of 1400x1050

System, Preferences,Screen Resolution.

It changed the modes lines to

Modes      "1400x1050"

Not as much fun as hacking the xorg.config file but it worked.

---

### Post by tseliot on 2007-02-18
Type "sudo nvidia-settings" and set your resolution from there

### Post by BrianZachary on 2007-10-04
My problem is very similar, but slightly different:
 
I installed Ubuntu with Wubi yesterday and I can't change the Screen resolution. First, it had only 2 options, 640x480 and 800x600 which is what it was set at. I use 1024x768 on my Windows and that's what I'm used to. I was looking for some kind of updates or something, but no luck. I added a bunch of programs with the Add/Remove utility and restarted and when it came back up, the Screen Resolution was set at 640x480 and I couldn't change it because that is now the only selection there. I thought about uninstalling and trying again, but if I can find a way to fix this problem first, I'd rather do that.
 
My friend installed Ubuntu on his computer with the Ubuntu installer you have to burn on disc. When it was finished it had about 119 updates. When those were done and it was back up, he had more choices for the Screen Resolution. When I got mine up I had about 120 some updates, did those but nothing changed with the Screen resolution.
 
Does anyone know how I can fix this? I don't know what differences you get between the 2 different ways to install Ubuntu, but it seems there must be something to it. Any help would be greatly appreciated.
 
My computer is an eMachines with Windows XP SP2 and my Display info is as follows:
 
NVIDIA GeForce4 MX Integrated GPU [Display adapter]
ACR AL1716 [Monitor] (17.1"vis, September 2005)
 
If anyone can help me it would bge greatly appreciated.
